thread.title: Fort Gay mayor arrested on multiple financial crime charges, including embezzlement
thread.title_full: Fort Gay mayor arrested on multiple financial crime charges, including embezzlement

author: LESLIE RUBIN
title: Fort Gay mayor arrested on multiple financial crime charges, including embezzlement
text: WAYNE COUNTY, W.Va. (WCHS) — The mayor of Fort Gay in Wayne County, along with the town's recorder and treasurer, have been arrested and accused of a long list of alleged financial crimes. Mayor Joetta Hatfield, 68, is charged with fraudulent schemes, embezzlement, falsifying accounts by a public employee, laundering more than $20,000 through financial transactions and conspiracy against the state. West Virginia State Police also arrested Sheila Bowen, 53, in connection with the investigation. Bowen serves as the town's treasurer and recorder. She is facing the exact charges as Hatfield. According to a criminal complaint filed in Wayne County Magistrate Court, state police were contacted by an investigator from the West Virginia State Auditor's Office in late October about information the auditor's office had received involving the two. State police said between March 2019 and May 2024, Hatfield and Bowen used their positions as elected officials for the town to embezzle money through "various schemes." "These schemes were subsequently obscured from the Fort Gay Town Council by omission and falsification of financial documents, upon which the Town Council would view and use to make financial decisions," Cpl. C.J. Eastridge said in the complaint. "This was possible because both Hatfield and Bowen possessed signature authority on town bank accounts and had access to all checkbooks and debit cards." Troopers said Bowen was responsible for recording all town accounts and preparing financial reports. During the same timeframe, Eastridge said the pair would routinely use checkbooks that belonged to various town accounts to write themselves checks that were later negotiated by the two, which were not authorized by the council, or supported by any documentation. Investigators went on to say that the payments consisted of 107 checks written to Hatfield in the amount totaling $96,425.20 and 103 checks written to Bowen in the amount of $94,540.14. Hatfield and Bowen would then sign the checks to fulfill the two-signature requirement of the checks, troopers said. Troopers said QuickBooks registers that were handed to councilmembers during meetings had the payments disguised within the registers as payments to various other vendors used by the town with arbitrary accounts associated with them. Troopers also said between Oct. 2022 and July 2024, Bowen used various Town of Fort Gay Water account numbers to pay her private power bills on 15 different occasions, totaling $8,572.75. "It should be noted that Sheila Bowen also admitted in a recorded audio statement that she did use Town of Fort Gay funds for her to pay these personal bills," Eastridge said. He went on to say that legitimate AEP payments for the town were present on the registers given to the town council while the personal payments were omitted. Between Oct. 2017 and May 2025, Bowen is accused of using the town's general account to pay her private AT&T phone bill on 53 occasions for a total of $16,036.64. Also, in Bowen's recorded statement, troopers said she told them she and Hatfield would meet in her office on Mondays and go over the QuickBooks registers and conspire to decide which payments to alter or omit. Bowen also said the QuickBook registers "were a mess" and that it may be impossible to figure out exactly what all had been altered, according to the complaint. Troopers said alterations or omissions had occurred on at least 207 occasions in the QuickBooks registers. The women also allegedly lowered other vendor costs to obscure the true cost. "It should be noted that alterations of amounts also occurred for purchases made at Hatfield's, a furniture store owned by Hatfield's husband," the complaint said. Hatfield is also accused of using the town's water works checking account and routing numbers to pay her private Frontier Communications services on 29 occasions for a total of $3,909.97. She also is accused of using various town accounts to pay her private AEP bills 14 different times for a total of $8,908.09. Both women's bonds were set at $25,000 and were posted. Calls to members of the town's council have not yet been returned.
